title: atriplex cinerea subsp. eucinerea
intro: Atriplex cinerea subsp. eucinerea, also known as the grey saltbush, is a species of shrub in the family Amaranthaceae. It is a plant native to Australia, used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The grey saltbush has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its leaves have been used to make infusions and salves to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Atriplex cinerea subsp. eucinerea can be made into teas, salves, and infusions to utilize its medicinal and health benefits.
